title: Reporting Emergencies
learningGoal: The learner will construct sentences to report medical symptoms, declare stolen items using the passive voice, and request emergency services.
instructions: Translate the English emergency phrases into Japanese. These are critical sentences to memorize.

contentItems.text: Pay close attention to particles (ga vs wo) and verb tenses.

writingPrompt: Translate: 1) My head hurts. 2) I caught a cold. 3) My wallet was stolen. 4) Please call the police.
promptGoal: To construct grammatically accurate emergency reports.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: あたまが いたいです。かぜを ひきました。さいふを ぬすまれました。けいさつを よんで ください。
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Atama ga itai desu. Kaze wo hikimashita. Saifu wo nusumaremashita. Keisatsu wo yonde kudasai.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: My head hurts. I caught a cold. My wallet was stolen. Please call the police.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect sentence construction with correct particles and verb forms.

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the learner use 'ga' with 'itai desu'?
Did the learner correctly write 'kaze wo hikimashita'?
Did the learner use the passive form 'nusumaremashita'?
Did the learner write 'keisatsu wo yonde kudasai'?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: あたまが いたいです。かぜを ひきました。さいふを ぬすまれました。けいさつを よんで ください。
acceptableAlternates: あたまがいたい。かぜをひいた。さいふをぬすまれた。けいさつをよんでください。
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Mastery of ga vs wo particles in emergency contexts.

feedbackRules.onPass: Brilliant writing! You correctly applied the emergency grammar structures.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the vocabulary list. 'Nusumaremashita' means stolen. 'Nakushimashita' means lost.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you use the 'ga' particle when saying a body part hurts (Atama ga itai).

hintText: Use 'wo' with 'nusumaremashita' because it happened to your object.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Saifu ga nusumaremashita'. It should be 'wo'.
